name: Delta-V
description: Analog dual AD function generator in 10HP. Per channel: Attack, Decay, Shape, one-shot/LFO switch, Attack+Decay CV, dedicated VCA (5V-normalized), attenuverter, EOC. Link switch and DC-coupled SUM. Envelopes, LFOs to ~1.7kHz, ASR via SLEW, mixing.

controls.name: Attack
controls.description: Envelope rise time — the time for the output to climb from 0V to peak after a trigger. In Cycle mode, sets the rising slope of the LFO.
controls.detail: Short to long; together with Decay reaches audio rate (~1.7kHz) in Cycle mode

controls.name: Decay
controls.description: Envelope fall time — how long the output takes to return to 0V after the attack peak. In Cycle mode, sets the falling slope.
controls.detail: Short to long; very long settings give envelopes over an hour

controls.name: Shape
controls.description: Bends the slope curve of both attack and decay stages.
controls.detail: CCW: exponential · noon: linear · CW: logarithmic

controls.name: Output Attenuverter
controls.description: Scales and optionally inverts the channel output. Sets envelope/LFO amplitude at OUT, and also sets this channel's contribution to the SUM bus.
controls.detail: -1× at full CCW · 0 at noon · +1× at full CW

controls.name: Cycle Switch (One-shot / LFO)
controls.description: Toggles between one-shot AD envelope (triggered by gate) and free-running LFO. In LFO mode the channel self-cycles; Attack+Decay set the waveshape and rate, and Trig still resets the cycle if patched.

controls.name: Trigger Link Switch
controls.description: Normals TRIG 1 to TRIG 2 so both channels fire from a single gate patched into TRIG 1. Turn off to drive each channel from its own trigger.
controls.detail: On: CH 1 Trig drives CH 2 · Off: independent

inputs.name: TRIG 1 / TRIG 2
inputs.description: Gate/trigger input per channel — starts the AD envelope or resets the LFO cycle.
inputs.normalizedTo: TRIG 2 is normalled from TRIG 1 when Link switch is on

inputs.name: SLEW 1 / SLEW 2
inputs.description: Gate input that is slewed by the Attack and Decay times — creates a classic ASR envelope: rises at Attack rate while the gate is high, holds as long as the gate stays high, falls at Decay rate when the gate drops.

inputs.name: A-CV 1 / A-CV 2
inputs.description: CV input for Attack time — modulate rise rate with an LFO, envelope, or sequencer.

inputs.name: D-CV 1 / D-CV 2
inputs.description: CV input for Decay time — modulate fall rate, useful for dynamic / accelerating envelope shapes.

inputs.name: VCA 1 / VCA 2
inputs.description: Audio or CV input to the per-channel internal VCA. The channel output becomes this signal amplified by the envelope/LFO. Normalled to +5V when unpatched, which is why OUT produces the raw envelope when nothing is plugged in.

outputs.name: OUT 1 / OUT 2
outputs.description: Single channel output per channel. With nothing in the VCA input: raw envelope/LFO at the level set by the attenuverter (±10V). With audio in the VCA input: that audio amplified by the envelope/LFO. With CV in the VCA input: envelope amplitude scaled by that CV (accents, dynamics).

outputs.name: EOC 1 / EOC 2
outputs.description: End-of-cycle trigger — pulses at the end of every decay stage (envelope or LFO mode). Handy for chaining envelopes or as a clock tap from an LFO.

outputs.name: SUM
outputs.description: DC-coupled mix of OUT 1 + OUT 2 via their output attenuverters. Usable with audio or CV — two-channel mixer or summed modulator.

behaviors.name: Cycle mode reaches audio rate
behaviors.description: Channel self-cycles as a continuous AD-shaped oscillator. Rate ranges from sub-Hz LFO to roughly 1.7kHz — short enough Attack+Decay turn the channel into a low-fi VCO whose waveform is bent by the Shape knob.

behaviors.name: SLEW is an ASR converter (gate-in)
behaviors.description: The SLEW input accepts gates and shapes them with the channel's Attack and Decay times, holding the peak while the gate is high. This turns a gate into a true attack-sustain-release envelope — not a general audio slew limiter.

behaviors.name: One jack, two roles (envelope or VCA)
behaviors.description: Because the VCA input is normalled to +5V, OUT delivers the raw envelope/LFO when nothing is patched in. Insert audio into the VCA input and the same OUT jack becomes the amplified (enveloped) audio. Insert CV and you get an attenuated / shaped CV.

behaviors.name: Bipolar sum mixer
behaviors.trigger: Both channels patched to inputs, levels set
behaviors.description: SUM is a two-channel DC-coupled mixer via the output attenuverters. Combine two envelopes, two LFOs, or two audio signals (one inverted, one not) into a single bus.

behaviors.name: Linked dual envelope
behaviors.trigger: Link switch on + single gate at TRIG 1
behaviors.description: Both channels fire from one trigger with independent Attack/Decay/Shape. Stack two envelopes — one snappy, one slow — for layered shapes from a single gate.

behaviors.name: EOC chaining
behaviors.description: End-of-cycle pulses are full triggers. EOC 1 → TRIG 2 builds sequenced bursts, ratchets, or complex LFOs where one cycle starts the next.

behaviors.name: CV over Attack and Decay
behaviors.description: Dedicated Attack and Decay CV inputs let velocity, LFOs, or even a channel's own OUT warp rise/fall times — enabling bouncing-ball decays and dynamic accents without an external attenuator.

patchIdeas.name: Two-voice AD (stereo voices in 10HP)
patchIdeas.patch: Each channel becomes a full voice: gate in, VCO in the VCA, OUT is the enveloped voice. Pan hard L/R for an instant stereo duo.
steps: Set both Cycle switches to one-shot (envelope mode).
CH 1: Attack short, Decay medium. CH 1 Attenuverter: full CW (+1× unity).
CH 2: Attack short, Decay long. CH 2 Attenuverter: full CW.
Patch gate source 1 → Delta-V TRIG 1.
Patch gate source 2 → Delta-V TRIG 2 (keep Link switch OFF).
Patch VCO 1 → Delta-V VCA 1. Patch VCO 2 → Delta-V VCA 2.
Patch Delta-V OUT 1 → Output L. Patch Delta-V OUT 2 → Output R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 and OUT 2 — enveloped audio, one voice per channel.
patchIdeas.hear: Two independent plucked/sustained voices, each with its own dedicated VCA and shape. Two full monosynths in a single 10HP module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Delta-V:
* CH 1: Cycle OFF · Attack short · Decay medium · Atten full CW
* CH 2: Cycle OFF · Attack short · Decay long · Atten full CW
* Link: OFF

VCO 1:

VCO 2:

- Gate 1 (Out) g> Delta-V (TRIG 1)
- Gate 2 (Out) g> Delta-V (TRIG 2)
- VCO 1 (Out) -> Delta-V (VCA 1)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> Delta-V (VCA 2)
- Delta-V (OUT 1) -> Output (In L)
- Delta-V (OUT 2) -> Output (In R)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate cycling VCO
patchIdeas.patch: Flip CH 1 to LFO mode with very short Attack+Decay and OUT 1 becomes a low-fi AD oscillator up to about 1.7kHz. Shape morphs the waveform.
steps: Set CH 1 Cycle switch to LFO.
Turn CH 1 Attack fully CCW (shortest).
Turn CH 1 Decay fully CCW (shortest).
Set Shape to noon for a linear triangle-ish wave; CCW for exponential; CW for logarithmic.
Set CH 1 Attenuverter full CW for full-level output.
Unpatch anything from VCA 1 (so OUT gives raw waveform, not amplified audio).
Patch Delta-V OUT 1 → Filter (In) → Output.
patchIdeas.hear: A buzzy, shape-morphing tone in the audible range. Sweep Decay slowly to hear the pitch/waveform blend smoothly from sub-audio clicks up to a sustained tone.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Delta-V:
* CH 1: Cycle LFO
* CH 1: Attack fully CCW
* CH 1: Decay fully CCW
* CH 1 Atten: full CW

- Delta-V (OUT 1) -> Filter (In) // audio-rate cycling
- Filter (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Gate → ASR envelope (via SLEW)
patchIdeas.patch: The SLEW input turns a gate into a classic attack-sustain-release envelope — rises at Attack, holds as long as the gate stays high, falls at Decay.
steps: Set CH 1 Cycle switch to one-shot (envelope mode).
Set CH 1 Attack to your desired rise time.
Set CH 1 Decay to your desired release time.
Set CH 1 Attenuverter full CW.
Patch gate source (e.g. sequencer gate) → Delta-V SLEW 1 (NOT TRIG 1).
Patch Delta-V OUT 1 → VCA CV (or filter cutoff).
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — full ASR envelope (0V to +10V), follows gate length.
patchIdeas.hear: The envelope rises to full while the gate is high, plateaus for the gate's duration, then falls when the gate drops. Long gates give sustain; short gates give AD-like plucks.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Delta-V:
* CH 1: Cycle OFF
* CH 1: Attack = attack time
* CH 1: Decay = release time

Seq:

- Seq (Gate) g> Delta-V (SLEW 1) // ASR via slew
- Delta-V (OUT 1) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Linked complex envelope (two shapes, one gate)
patchIdeas.patch: Link switch fires both channels from TRIG 1. Sum a fast bite + slow body into one modulation source via the SUM output.
steps: Turn the Link switch ON.
Set both Cycle switches to one-shot (envelope).
CH 1: Attack very short, Decay short (the bite). CH 1 Atten: ~80% CW.
CH 2: Attack medium, Decay long (the body). CH 2 Atten: ~60% CW.
Patch single gate → Delta-V TRIG 1 only.
Leave OUT 1 and OUT 2 unpatched (or monitor if you like).
Patch Delta-V SUM → Filter (Cutoff).
patchIdeas.hear: A filter sweep with snap up front and a slow release tail — like a two-stage envelope you'd build on DUSG or MATHS, but from a single gate.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Delta-V:
* Link: ON
* CH 1: Cycle OFF · Attack very short · Decay short · Atten ~80% CW
* CH 2: Cycle OFF · Attack medium · Decay long · Atten ~60% CW

- Gate (Out) g> Delta-V (TRIG 1)
- Delta-V (SUM) >> Filter (Cutoff) // bite + body

patchIdeas.name: Bouncing-ball envelope (self-modulated decay)
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 free-runs as an LFO, and its own OUT drives its Decay CV — each cycle shortens the fall, giving the characteristic accelerating bounce.
steps: Set CH 1 Cycle switch to LFO.
Set CH 1 Attack fully CCW (short).
Set CH 1 Decay to around 10 o'clock (medium-short) — this is the starting bounce rate.
Set CH 1 Attenuverter to about 3 o'clock (positive).
Unpatch VCA 1 so OUT 1 gives the raw envelope.
Patch Delta-V OUT 1 → Delta-V D-CV 1 (decay CV).
Patch Delta-V OUT 1 → VCA (CV) to hear it.
Trim the CH 1 attenuverter: more positive = faster bounce, less = slower.
patchIdeas.hear: An envelope that gets faster and faster on each repetition until it chatters, then stalls. Back off the attenuverter and it settles into a steady LFO.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Delta-V:
* CH 1: Cycle LFO · Attack CCW · Decay ~10 o'clock · Atten ~3 o'clock

- Delta-V (OUT 1) >> Delta-V (D-CV 1) // feedback shortens decay
- Delta-V (OUT 1) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Dual drifting LFO for stereo
patchIdeas.patch: Both channels free-run at slightly different rates — a classic stereo chorus/pan motion source.
steps: Set both Cycle switches to LFO.
CH 1: Attack and Decay at ~2 o'clock (slow).
CH 2: Attack and Decay slightly different from CH 1 (say ~2:30) so the two drift.
Set both Attenuverters full CW.
Unpatch both VCA inputs (so OUT gives raw LFO).
Patch Delta-V OUT 1 → VCA L (CV).
Patch Delta-V OUT 2 → VCA R (CV).
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 and OUT 2 — two drifting bipolar LFOs, ±10V.
patchIdeas.hear: A slow, unsynced stereo wobble — the two sides breathe against each other, never landing in phase, perfect for pad movement.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Delta-V:
* CH 1: Cycle LFO · slow
* CH 2: Cycle LFO · slightly slower

VCA L:

VCA R:

- Delta-V (OUT 1) >> VCA L (CV)
- Delta-V (OUT 2) >> VCA R (CV)

patchIdeas.name: EOC ratchet (burst drums)
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 in LFO mode machine-guns short pulses; each EOC fires CH 2, which plays a percussive envelope into a drum/VCA.
steps: Set CH 1 Cycle switch to LFO. Attack and Decay both short (~9 o'clock).
Set CH 2 Cycle switch to one-shot. CH 2 Attack very short, Decay short.
Set CH 2 Attenuverter full CW.
Patch gate → Delta-V TRIG 1 to start/stop bursts (optional; can leave CH 1 running).
Patch Delta-V EOC 1 → Delta-V TRIG 2.
Patch drum trigger / VCA CV input → Delta-V OUT 2.
Tweak CH 1 Decay to change ratchet rate.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 2 — percussive envelope bursts synced to CH 1's cycle rate.
patchIdeas.hear: Machine-gun ratcheted hits. Faster CH 1 Decay = quicker rolls; slower = tape-stop style slowdowns.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Delta-V:
* CH 1: Cycle LFO · Attack ~9 o'clock · Decay ~9 o'clock
* CH 2: Cycle OFF · Attack very short · Decay short · Atten full CW

Drum:

- Delta-V (EOC 1) t> Delta-V (TRIG 2)
- Delta-V (OUT 2) >> Drum (Level) // ratcheted bursts

patchIdeas.name: Two-channel DC mixer (via SUM)
patchIdeas.patch: With Attack and Decay at minimum and no triggers, each channel acts as a straight attenuverter — SUM becomes a 2-in DC-coupled mixer for CV or audio.
steps: Set both Cycle switches to one-shot.
Set CH 1 Attack and Decay fully CCW. Same for CH 2.
Do NOT patch anything into TRIG 1 or TRIG 2 (channels stay idle).
Patch source A → Delta-V VCA 1. Patch source B → Delta-V VCA 2.

Because the envelope is at 0V when untriggered, this trick works best as a CV/audio mixer by instead patching the two sources into SLEW 1 and SLEW 2 with gates held high, OR use it on output-coupled signals — see note below.

Simpler route: patch CV source A → Delta-V VCA 1 and source B → Delta-V VCA 2, then briefly hold a gate into TRIG 1+2 (Link ON) to open both envelopes to steady state; use CH 1 Atten positive and CH 2 Atten negative.
Patch Delta-V SUM → Output (In).
patchIdeas.signalOut: SUM — summed / difference of the two channel outputs.
patchIdeas.hear: CH 1 Atten positive and CH 2 Atten negative gives A minus B at the SUM. Both positive sums them. Works cleanly on audio because SUM is DC-coupled and the attenuverters are bipolar.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Delta-V:
* CH 1: Cycle OFF · Attack/Decay min · Atten +70% CW
* CH 2: Cycle OFF · Attack/Decay min · Atten -50% (CCW)
* Link: ON (sustain both envelopes open)

Osc A:

Osc B:

- Osc A (Out) -> Delta-V (VCA 1)
- Osc B (Out) -> Delta-V (VCA 2)
- Sustained Gate (Out) g> Delta-V (TRIG 1)
- Delta-V (SUM) -> Output (In) // A minus B
